Comment rules: Added missing using directives for Aspose.Words.Tables, Aspose.Words.Drawing, Aspose.Words.Fields, and Aspose.Words.Comments to resolve Table, Shape, FieldDate, and Comment types.
Ensured FootnoteType, Footnote, Table, Shape, FieldDate, Comment, HeaderFooterType, and other element classes are referenced from the correct namespaces.
Add missing using directives for Aspose.Words.Drawing, Aspose.Words.Tables, Aspose.Words.Fields, and Aspose.Words.Comments.
Use the correct enum and class names: FootnoteType, Shape, ShapeType, Table, Footnote, FieldDate, Comment.
Create the original document and populate it with footnotes, tables, textboxes, fields, comments, and headers as shown in the API example.
Table rules: Add required using directives for Aspose.Words namespaces (Drawing, Fields, Tables, Comparing).
Removed unused types (FootnoteType, Shape, Table, FieldDate, etc.) that caused compile errors.
Range rules: Cast the field retrieved from docEdited.Range.Fields[0] to FieldDate before setting UseLunarCalendar.
Document merge/insert rules: Clone the original document and modify each element to simulate an edited version.
Loading rules: If you need to specify the format explicitly, create a LoadOptions instance: new LoadOptions(LoadFormat.Mhtml) and pass it as the second argument.
Saving/Export rules: Import Aspose.Words.Saving to access PdfSaveOptions and RevisionOptions.
Create a PdfSaveOptions instance and assign a RevisionOptions object to its RevisionOptions property.
Set ShowInsertedContent, ShowDeletedContent, and HighlightColor (or other desired properties) on RevisionOptions to control how revisions appear in the PDF.
Pass the PdfSaveOptions instance to Document.Save instead of using the SaveFormat enum overload.
Save the resulting document that contains revision marks.
Save the result using the same .mht extension to retain MHTML output.
